commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an RFC and extends the runtime plugin lock/build pipeline to materialize shrinkwrapped OpenClaw catalog artifacts, promoting acpx, codex, copilot, and memory-lancedb as supported runtimePlugins while keeping raw npm/ClawHub specs out of user config.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a packaging feature PR rather than a bug report. The relevant checks are package-contract and generated-lock validation, which the PR body reports for Darwin while Linux still needs CI proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This collaborator draft has no narrow automated repair; maintainers should finish platform CI review and decide whether the broader shrinkwrap/ClawHub updater path is ready to land.

target.title: feat: materialize shrinkwrapped runtime plugins
target.body:
# Human written summary:
The intent of this change is, as written by a human:
> all users want is to be able to use nix-openclaw with plugins the same way that they do with upstream openclaw, but declaratively. its that simple. we want to figure out how to implement that here in simplest possible way but no simpler; with a minimum of retarded shit and hacks

_The rest of this PR was written by GPT-5 Codex-unknown, running in the codex harness. Full environment + prompt history appear at the end._

for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels

# Changes
- Adds RFC 2 for runtime plugin artifact materialization.
- Extends the generated runtime plugin lock pipeline to admit package roots whose runtime deps are materializer-valid through `npm-shrinkwrap.json` and `fetchNpmDeps`.
- Adds ClawHub npm-pack artifact resolution at lock update time, with fixed tarball hash/integrity validation and fail-closed materialization checks.
- Keeps the user API unchanged: supported catalog plugins are still selected with `programs.openclaw.runtimePlugins = [ "id" ];`.
- Regenerates OpenClaw v2026.6.1 runtime plugin locks: `acpx`, `codex`, `copilot`, and `memory-lancedb` are now supported in `dependencyMode = "shrinkwrap"`.
- Leaves current WhatsApp, Matrix, and Tlon unsupported because their shrinkwraps still cause offline npm to request uncached registry packages.

# Tests
- `node --check nix/scripts/update-openclaw-runtime-plugin-locks.mjs && node --check nix/scripts/check-openclaw-runtime-plugin-locks.mjs && node --check nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-plugin-install.mjs && node --check nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-plugin-prepare-npm.mjs` passed.
- `./nix/scripts/update-openclaw-runtime-plugin-locks.mjs --check` passed: 31 supported, 7 skipped.
- `nix build .#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-plugin-locks` passed.
- `nix build .#packages.aarch64-darwin.openclaw-runtime-plugin-memory-lancedb` passed.
- `nix build .#packages.aarch64-darwin.openclaw-runtime-plugin-acpx .#packages.aarch64-darwin.openclaw-runtime-plugin-codex .#packages.aarch64-darwin.openclaw-runtime-plugin-copilot` passed.
- `nix build .#packages.aarch64-darwin.openclaw-runtime-plugin-slack .#packages.aarch64-darwin.openclaw-runtime-plugin-discord` passed.
- `nix build .#checks.aarch64-darwin.config-validity .#checks.aarch64-darwin.gateway-smoke` passed.
- `nix build .#checks.aarch64-darwin.ci` passed.
- Local ClawSweeper-style preflight passed after fixing the unsupported-id fixture and ClawHub integrity edge case.

# Risks
- Linux CI still needs to prove platform-specific npm materialization for the new shrinkwrapped packages.
- The lock updater is intentionally more expensive because it runs npm dependency hashing and temporary Nix materialization proofs.
- ClawHub support is resolver-ready but current WhatsApp and Matrix artifacts remain unsupported until their shrinkwraps materialize offline.

# Follow-ups
- Decide separately whether nix-openclaw should ever rewrite inconsistent shrinkwrap dependency ranges; this PR deliberately does not.
- If upstream fixes WhatsApp, Matrix, or Tlon shrinkwraps, rerun the generator and let the same materialization gate promote them.

# Prompt History

## Environment
Harness: codex
Model: unknown
Thinking level: unknown
Terminal: unknown
System: macOS 26.5

## Prompts
| ISO-8601 | Prompt |
| --- | --- |
| 2026-06-05T16:20:00+02:00 | `Goal: design the next architecture step for nix-openclaw support of OpenClaw's upstream runtime npm/ClawHub plugin model. Do not patch repos yet. Do not create worktrees.` |
| 2026-06-05T16:40:00+02:00 | `all users want is to be able to use nix-openclaw with plugins the same way that they do with upstream openclaw, but declaratively. its that simple. we want to figure out how to implement that here in simplest possible way but no simpler; with a minimum of retarded shit and hacks` |
| 2026-06-05T16:55:00+02:00 | `what about clawhub does that shrinkwrap? the poitn isn't security the point is pacakging. if they have shrinkwwrapping then we have immutable installs OOTB and its easy, if not, then fuck off. right? go back to overall goal for the session: how do we make this work for our original north star of users being able to install plugins same way as in upstream?` |
| 2026-06-05T17:05:00+02:00 | `sure thats fine. okay then i think we have everyhting, simple as possible user config, as few knobs as possible, we support everything that has shrinkwrapped, we have a config, etc, this should be clear?` |
| 2026-06-05T17:05:30+02:00 | `lets create a worktree for implementing this and PR it. lets use [$session-goal-writer](/nix/store/hcks99ivrjhg2pg09lc97cqhd2ixh1w1-source/skills/session-goal-writer/SKILL.md) , success should be: written RFC that passes adversarial review (step one)) written execplan that passes adversarial review (step two) and written implementation and PR with green CI that also passes clawsweeper rubrics (locally [$clawsweeper-preflight](/Users/josh/.codex/skills/clawsweeper-preflight/SKILL.md) ) and remotely. is that enough info to start implementation while i go cook dinner?` |
